questions.question: Explain the working of Bubble Sort with an example array [5, 1, 4, 2, 8]. Show one complete pass.
keyPoints: Compare adjacent elements
Swap if left > right
After first pass, largest element (8) moves to end
Explain the comparison and swapping process

questions.question: What is the difference between stable and unstable sorting algorithms? Give one example of each.
keyPoints: Stable preserves relative order of equal elements
Unstable may change relative order
Example of stable: Merge Sort
Example of unstable: Quick Sort

questions.question: Write the algorithm for Selection Sort and explain why it makes exactly (n-1) swaps.
keyPoints: Algorithm: find minimum, swap with first unsorted position
Always makes n-1 swaps regardless of input
Each iteration places one element in final position
No best case optimization possible

questions.question: Explain the divide-and-conquer approach used in Merge Sort with the recurrence relation.
keyPoints: Divide: split array into two halves
Conquer: recursively sort both halves
Combine: merge sorted halves
Recurrence: T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n)

questions.question: What is the best case and worst case time complexity of Quick Sort? Explain when each case occurs.
keyPoints: Best case: O(n log n) when pivot divides array evenly
Worst case: O(n²) when pivot is smallest or largest
Worst case occurs for already sorted or reverse sorted arrays
Can be improved by choosing good pivot (median-of-three)

questions.question: Compare Bubble Sort, Selection Sort, and Insertion Sort in terms of time complexity and stability.
keyPoints: All three have O(n²) worst case
Bubble and Insertion are stable; Selection is unstable
Insertion Sort is best for nearly sorted data
Selection Sort has minimum number of swaps

questions.question: What is the purpose of the pivot element in Quick Sort? Describe the partition procedure.
keyPoints: Pivot is used to divide array into two sub-arrays
Elements less than pivot go to left
Elements greater than pivot go to right
Partition can be done using different schemes (Lomuto, Hoare)

questions.question: Write a complete algorithm for Merge Sort. Explain how the merge procedure works and analyze its time and space complexity.
keyPoints: Algorithm: divide, recursive sort, merge
Merge procedure compares elements from two halves
Merges into auxiliary array, then copies back
Time: O(n log n) in all cases
Space: O(n) auxiliary space required

questions.question: Describe the Quick Sort algorithm with a suitable example. Explain the importance of choosing a good pivot and discuss how to handle the worst-case scenario.
keyPoints: Algorithm uses divide-and-conquer with partitioning
Example showing partition steps
Good pivot selection (random, median-of-three)
Worst case occurs for sorted input
Solution: randomize array or use median-of-three

questions.question: Explain the working of Insertion Sort. When is it most efficient and why? Also explain why it is considered better than Bubble Sort and Selection Sort in practice.
keyPoints: Works like sorting playing cards in hand
Efficient for small and nearly sorted arrays
Best case O(n) when already sorted
In-place, stable, and adaptive
Outperforms other O(n²) algorithms in practice for small n

questions.question: Compare and contrast Merge Sort and Quick Sort. Discuss their advantages, disadvantages, and suitable use cases.
keyPoints: Both use divide-and-conquer approach
Merge Sort: stable, O(n log n) always, O(n) space
Quick Sort: unstable, O(n²) worst case, in-place
Quick Sort better for arrays due to cache locality
Merge Sort better for linked lists and external sorting
